description:
GOAL: Optimize hot calls by fusing adjacent frames into one Cranelift fn — no allocation for fused calls. Performance-load-bearing for v1.

## Decisions captured

- IN SCOPE for v1, NOT deferred (user decision: "i think we should inline"). Naive frame-per-call is too slow for tight loops.
- Operate on fz-IR BEFORE Cranelift lowering. Produces a "merged" fn whose body = caller body with callee body spliced where the call was.
- Roots: across an inlined call boundary, GC must still find live values. Since the merged fn has one frame (or no frame, if locals are register-resident), the inliner must compute the combined frame schema correctly.
- Static decision (NOT tier-up-driven). At codegen time we decide what to inline.

## Deliverables (new src/ir_inline.rs or extend src/fz_ir.rs)

- IR inliner pass over fz-IR Module.
- Inlining heuristic. Start simple:
  - Always inline leaf fns ≤ N IR stmts (N = 8? tune later).
  - Inline a fn if it has only one callsite (and isn't recursive).
  - More aggressive heuristics later.
- Frame schema recomputation post-inline: combined live-set of caller + callee, deduped.
- Don't inline across tier boundaries (interp-only fns) or across modules (reduces invalidation if fn changes — though for AOT this matters less).
- Recursion: don't inline self-recursive directly; can do bounded unrolling separately.

## Tests

- After inlining, count(100000, 0) doesn't allocate per-call (tail-recursion + single merged frame — already handled by .11.8 tail-call frame reuse, but inlining the body further removes call overhead).
- Hot loops show no per-iter allocation.
- GC still finds correct roots in inlined code (allocate during a hot loop, GC, verify both inlined and non-inlined paths see live values).
- Inliner doesn't introduce code bloat beyond a threshold (some sanity check).

## Risks

- Inliner correctness: must not violate liveness invariants or frame layouts.
- Inliner too aggressive: code bloat -> instruction cache pressure.

## Open

- Inline budget (max IR stmts to copy per call).
- Per-fn opt-out attribute (e.g. @no_inline)?
- Whole-program vs. per-fn inlining strategy.
title: Frame inliner (performance optimization)
